author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXXII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To David himself; for understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To David himself; for understanding; by which it is understood that not by the merits of works, but by the grace of God, man is delivered, confessing his sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed are they whose unrighteousness is forgiven, and whose sins are covered” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1): and whose sins are buried in oblivion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is the man to whom the Lord hath not imputed sin, nor is there guile in his mouth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2): nor has he in his mouth boastings of righteousness, when his conscience is full of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because I kept silence, my bones waxed old:” because I made not with my mouth “confession unto salvation,” all firmness in me has grown old in infirmity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Through my roaring all the day long” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3): when I was ungodly and a blasphemer, crying against God, as though defending and excusing my sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because day and night Thy Hand was heavy upon me:” because, through the continual punishment of Thy scourges, “I was turned in misery, while a thorn was fixed through me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): I was made miserable by knowing my misery, being pricked with an evil conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I acknowledged my sin, and my unrighteousness have I not hid:” that is, my unrighteousness have I not concealed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I said, I will confess against myself my unrighteousness to the Lord:” I said, I will confess, not against God (as in my ungodly crying, when I kept silence), but against myself, my unrighteousness to the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou forgavest the iniquity of my heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5); hearing the word of confession in the heart, before it was uttered with the voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For this shall every one that is holy pray unto Thee in an acceptable time:” for this wickedness of heart shall every one that is righteous pray unto Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not by their own merits will they be holy, but by that acceptable time, that is, at His coming, who redeemed us from sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nevertheless in the flood of great waters they shall not come nigh him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6): nevertheless, let none think, when the end has come suddenly, as in the days of Noah, that there remaineth a place of confession, whereby he may draw nigh unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art my refuge from the pressures, which have compassed me about:” Thou art my refuge from the pressure of my sins, which hath compassed my heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Thou, my Rejoicing, deliver me from them that compass me about” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 7): in Thee is my joy: deliver me from the sorrow which my sins bring upon me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The answer of God: “I will give thee understanding, and will set thee in the way in which thou shalt go;” I will give thee understanding after confession, that thou depart not from the way in which thou shouldest go; lest thou wish to be in thine own power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will fix Mine Eyes upon thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8); so will make sure upon thee My Love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be not ye like unto horse or mule, which have no understanding:” and therefore would govern themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But saith the Prophet, “Hold in their jaws with bit and bridle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do Thou then, O God, unto them “that will not come nigh Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9), what man doth to horse and mule, that by scourges Thou make them to bear Thy rule.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Many are the scourges of the sinner:” much is he scourged, who, confessing not his sins to God, would be his own ruler.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But he that trusteth in the Lord, mercy compasseth him about” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10); but he that trusteth in the Lord, and submitteth himself to His rule, mercy shall compass him about.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be glad in the Lord, and rejoice, ye righteous:” be glad, and rejoice, ye righteous, not in yourselves, but in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And glory, all ye that are right in heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11): and glory in Him, all ye who understand that it is right to be subject unto Him, that so ye may be placed above all things beside.
